patterns.rationale: Generic "Vitamin K" defaulting to K1 (phylloquinone) is the standard regulatory assumption.
Tomato extract products deliver lycopene as the active. Lycopene canonical has the RCT evidence.
Ca ascorbate label headed by Calcium — user bought it for Ca primarily.

patterns.rationale: Same root canonical; resolver dropped the "_extract" suffix. No score loss.
High-absorption Curcumin products are 95% curcuminoids — should be curcumin, not generic turmeric.
DCP-sourced Phosphorus row — exact pattern called out as fixed in code docstring, still happening.
Broccoli sprout extracts are dosed for sulforaphane content.
Inositol hexanicotinate is genuinely a niacin form (delivers niacin systemically).

patterns.rationale: NR is a niacin family member.
Fish oil is the source; DHA is the molecule. Losing DHA-specificity costs evidence depth.

patterns.rationale: Guarana products are typically standardized for caffeine content.
Papain (enzyme) routed to papaya fruit. Depends on label.
Branded → generic for the same molecule.

patterns.rationale: Cross-domain — likely form-alias collision (no biological relationship).
Mg ascorbate label headed by Magnesium — user bought it for Mg, not C.
Leucine label routed to casein because leucine is a casein-derived amino acid.
Sunflower oil is the SOURCE, not the form. Vit E α-tocopherol is the nutrient.
Same family — K-phosphate row tracked as K, not P.
When label is generic turmeric BUT form is curcumin extract 95%, curcumin canonical is more accurate.
Risk canonical routed to safe canonical — needs safety/regulatory review.
